hypotheses.title: Pourbaix-Quantified Fe-S Cluster Displacement — The Eh Window of Cuproptotic Lethality
hypotheses.bridge_type: Cu-Fe sulfide displacement chemistry embedded in Eh-pH speciation framework
hypotheses.key_prediction: Ligand-extended Pourbaix places Cu2+/Cu+ boundary at -260±30 mV; CIA overexpression delays cuproptosis ≥2h while LIAS overexpression delays ≤30min; LIAS-associated Fe-S clusters destroyed before CIA-scaffolded clusters

hypotheses.title: FDX1 as Calibrated Kinetic Gate With Elesclomol Speciation — Predict-Then-Measure Pourbaix Validation
hypotheses.bridge_type: Quantitative Eh-pH correspondence between vent geochemistry and mitochondrial copper redox kinetics
hypotheses.key_prediction: FDX1 second-shell mutants show sigmoidal cuproptosis sensitivity curve with inflection at E0'=-260±20 mV matching Pourbaix boundary; elesclomol-to-lipoyl Cu transfer K=0.8±0.5

hypotheses.title: Metallothionein-CuS Thermodynamic Isomorphism — A Unified Sulfur-Coordination Framework for Cuproptosis Resistance
hypotheses.bridge_type: Structural and thermodynamic isomorphism between biological Cu+-thiolate buffering (MT) and geochemical Cu+-sulfide buffering (CuS/covellite)
hypotheses.key_prediction: MT Cu-S coordination (2.24±0.03 Å, trigonal) within 0.05 Å of covellite; graded MT overexpression shifts cuproptosis EC50 proportionally to MT Cu-binding capacity; Eh-dependent MT Cu release at -150 mV

hypotheses.title: Cuproptosis Fe-S Displacement as Evolutionary Selection Pressure — Genomic Signatures in Cu-Tolerant Organisms
hypotheses.bridge_type: Evolutionary co-selection driven by Cu-Fe sulfide displacement chemistry
hypotheses.key_prediction: FDX1 shows ≥5-fold higher Cu2+ reductase kcat/Km than other ferredoxins of similar E0'; D136/D139 show dN/dS>1 in Cu-rich habitat organisms; FDX1-LIAS operon proximity shorter in Cu-rich vs Cu-poor prokaryotes

hypotheses.title: Methanobactin-Lipoyl Structural Convergence — Cyclic Dithiolate Cu+ Chelation as Universal Copper Management Motif
hypotheses.bridge_type: Structural convergence of cyclic dithiolate Cu+ coordination between vent chalkophore and cuproptosis Cu-target chemistry
hypotheses.key_prediction: Cyclic dithiolate Cu+ affinity peaks at ring size 5-6 (log K≥15); exogenous methanobactin protects against cuproptosis (EC50 increase ≥3-fold); mbnA-knockout methanotrophs phenocopy cuproptosis Fe-S cluster loss
